title: linum perenne var. boreale
intro: Linum perenne var. boreale, also known as Northern Flax or Blue Flax, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the genus Linum and is native to the northern regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant is rich in mucilage, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Linum perenne var. boreale can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
